title: Fraud Detection AWS Platform
shortDescription: Fraud transaction scoring platform on AWS — a Python worker in private EC2 subnets with zero inbound exposure, SSM-only access, and a full SQS → EC2 → DynamoDB pipeline.
longDescription: A fraud transaction scoring platform built on AWS with a Python worker running in private EC2 subnets — no SSH, no bastion, zero inbound exposure. Operator access is exclusively through SSM Session Manager over VPC Interface Endpoints. The processing pipeline runs SQS → EC2 → DynamoDB, provisioned by 11 single-responsibility Terraform modules and deployed via OIDC-based GitHub Actions CI/CD with no static credentials (a dual-role read-only-plan / admin-apply model scoped to exact OIDC sub claims, with terraform plan posted as a PR comment). Hardened AL2023 ARM64 Golden AMIs are produced by an EC2 Image Builder pipeline with a CIS baseline and an ARM64 validation gate before ASG rolling updates, and security/observability is wired through GuardDuty, AWS Backup, and EventBridge → SNS → Lambda → Slack alerting.

architecture.summary: A Python scoring worker runs in private EC2 subnets behind an Auto Scaling group, consuming from SQS and writing scored results to DynamoDB. There is no public ingress — operators reach instances only through SSM Session Manager over VPC Interface Endpoints. Hardened ARM64 Golden AMIs flow from an EC2 Image Builder pipeline with a CIS baseline and a validation gate before ASG rolling updates. GuardDuty, AWS Backup, and EventBridge → SNS → Lambda → Slack provide threat detection, recovery, and alerting.
architecture.diagramAlt: Fraud Detection Platform AWS architecture: a Python worker in private EC2 subnets behind an Auto Scaling group consuming from SQS and writing to DynamoDB, SSM-only access over VPC Interface Endpoints, an EC2 Image Builder Golden AMI pipeline, GitHub Actions OIDC CI/CD, and GuardDuty / EventBridge / SNS / Lambda alerting.

operationalValue.problemSolved: Scores fraud transactions on infrastructure with zero inbound exposure and no static credentials anywhere in the delivery path.
outcomes: Zero inbound exposure — no SSH, no bastion; SSM-only operator access over VPC Interface Endpoints
11 single-responsibility Terraform modules with OIDC-based CI/CD (no static credentials)
Hardened AL2023 ARM64 Golden AMIs gated by an ARM64 validation step before reaching the fleet
HIGH/CRITICAL GuardDuty findings routed to Slack via EventBridge → SNS → Lambda

metrics.reliabilityImpact: AWS Backup with a 7-day warm / 30-day cold KMS-encrypted vault

screenshots.caption: Least-privilege IAM — the workload role carries AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore plus two scoped inline policies.

title: Kubernetes Homelab Platform
shortDescription: Multi-node Kubernetes cluster provisioned from scratch with Vagrant and VirtualBox — CKA preparation with production-grade operational practices.
longDescription: Multi-node K8s cluster built from scratch to develop production-grade operational skills. Covers CNI configuration, RBAC, scheduling, persistent storage, and pod lifecycle management.

architecture.summary: Multi-node cluster with dedicated control plane and worker nodes. CNI configured for pod networking, RBAC enforced from day one, persistent storage via hostPath and NFS.

operationalValue.problemSolved: Hands-on platform for developing production Kubernetes skills without cloud cost.
outcomes: CKA exam preparation with real workloads
Operational runbooks tested against real failure scenarios

title: AWS CloudWatch Observability Stack
shortDescription: Production CloudWatch observability implementation with custom dashboards, composite alarms, Container Insights for EKS, and log-based alerting.
longDescription: End-to-end observability stack built on CloudWatch — custom metrics, composite alarms, Container Insights dashboards for EKS, and log-based alerting. Designed to reduce MTTD for production incidents.

architecture.summary: Layered observability: infrastructure metrics → application metrics → log insights → composite alarms → SNS notifications. Container Insights provides EKS cluster and pod-level visibility.

operationalValue.problemSolved: Reduces mean time to detection from hours to minutes by surfacing infrastructure anomalies before they impact end users.
outcomes: Composite alarms reduce alert noise by 60%
Container Insights provides pod-level resource attribution
Log Insights queries replace manual log analysis

metrics.reliabilityImpact: MTTD reduced from hours to minutes

title: Container Runtime Runbooks — Homelabs
shortDescription: Operational runbooks for container runtime troubleshooting in homelab Kubernetes environments — covering containerd, CRI-O, Docker, and pod lifecycle failures.
longDescription: A collection of production-grade runbooks for diagnosing and resolving container runtime issues in Kubernetes homelabs. Covers containerd crashes, image pull failures, CRI socket errors, OOMKill debugging, and node-level runtime recovery.

architecture.summary: Runbook library organized by failure domain: runtime crashes, image pull errors, CRI socket issues, OOMKill events, and node recovery procedures.

operationalValue.problemSolved: Eliminates time spent rediscovering container runtime diagnostics during homelab incidents.
outcomes: Structured runbooks for 10+ common runtime failure scenarios
Consistent diagnostic approach across containerd, CRI-O, and Docker
CKA exam preparation through real operational documentation
